source_pr.title: test: fix unstable TestMemoryLeak
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #28049

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

increase the repeat times and memory limit, to tolerate unstable GC(?)

How it Works:

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted on heap/memory usage deltas that are sensitive to Go GC timing and heap reuse behavior (runtime.GC() does not guarantee memory will be reclaimed to a stable level within a fixed window). This PR mitigates the flake by increasing the number of init/close iterations and relaxing the heap-in-use upper bound (150MiB→300MiB) to better tolerate GC variability under CI.
fix_pattern: Relax memory-leak assertion bounds to tolerate GC timing/heap reuse variability under CI (increase iterations and raise the heap-in-use delta threshold).
